title: Mental Health Therapy Informed Consent
description: An informed consent document signed by clinical counseling or psychotherapy clients outlining confidentiality exceptions and emergency protocols.
whatsCovered: Therapeutic methods, diagnostic evaluations, and target goals
Strict confidentiality limits (abuse, self-harm, court orders)
Hourly session rates and billing terms
Emergency contact instructions and crisis line information
whenToUse: Use this consent form when onboarding a new patient for clinical psychology, counseling, or outpatient psychotherapy services.

clauses.heading: PSYCHOTHERAPY INFORMED CONSENT
clauses.body: This Informed Consent is entered into by {patient_name} ("Client"), in favor of {therapist_name} ("Therapist"), on {effective_date}.

clauses.heading: 1. Therapeutic Approach
clauses.body: Therapist provides clinical evaluation, cognitive behavioral therapy, or psychotherapeutic counseling. Client understands that therapy requires active participation and can sometimes bring up difficult or painful feelings.

clauses.heading: 2. Limits of Confidentiality
clauses.body: All discussions, files, and progress logs are confidential. However, Therapist is legally required to breach confidentiality in the event of: (a) suspicion of abuse or neglect of a child, elder, or vulnerable adult; (b) clear threats of self-harm or suicide; (c) clear threats of violence to others; or (d) formal judicial court subpoenas.

clauses.heading: 3. Fees & Cancel Rules
clauses.body: Client agrees to pay Therapist a flat fee of {session_rate} per 50-minute session. Client must provide at least 24 hours' notice to cancel or reschedule. Late cancellations shall be billed the full session rate.

clauses.heading: 4. Crisis Support Disclaimer
clauses.body: Therapist is not available for immediate emergency crisis support. In the event of a mental health emergency, Client agrees to call 911 or visit the nearest hospital emergency room.

clauses.heading: 5. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Consent shall be governed by, and interpreted in accordance with, the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Can I stop therapy at any time?
faq.answer: Yes. You have the right to withdraw from clinical treatment or seek a referral at any time.

faq.question: Does my health insurance cover therapy?
faq.answer: Many plans cover outpatient psychotherapy. The clinic can provide billing statements (Superbills) for you to submit to your insurance.
